picks.title: Festival TREK – Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Netherlands' largest travelling food festival. Dozens of food trucks & stalls, live music, theatre—transforming the park into an open-air restaurant.
picks.why: Free entry, street food from multiple cuisines, live enterta
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam, Festival Trek official site

picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: Waterfront food, drink, and culture pop-up festival with street food, drinks, and local vendors.

picks.title: TREK Festival
picks.blurb: Food, drink, and music festival spanning the weekend with pop-ups and activities.

picks.title: TREK Food Festival
picks.blurb: Open-air food festival with international street food trucks, pop-up restaurants, and local artisan stalls.

picks.title: Amsterdam Yard Sale Braderie
picks.blurb: 100 market stalls of vintage, secondhand, and local goods from Amsterdam residents.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · De Hortus

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: Global pop superstar's first stadium tour supporting fourth album 'The Romantic'; opening acts include DJ Pee .Wee.
picks.source: Songkick · Amsterdam Tips / Songkick · I amsterdam (via Ticketmaster)

picks.title: Julidans Festival
picks.blurb: International contemporary dance festival with innovative choreography and global perspectives. Final days of the festival.
picks.why: World-class choreography, last weekend to catch performances

picks.title: De Amsterdamse Zomer
picks.blurb: Three-hour shows with Netherlands' biggest pop & folk artists at the Olympic Stadium. Upbeat sing-alongs and surprise duets.
picks.why: Major Dutch stars, ultimate summer festival feeling

picks.title: Julidans
picks.blurb: International contemporary dance festival across multiple Amsterdam venues, showcasing innovative choreography and movement.
picks.why: Runs through the weekend, cutting-edge dance programming

picks.title: Wonderfeel
picks.blurb: 3-day classical & jazz festival with 400+ musicians, 100+ shows across baroque to contemporary.
picks.why: 400 musicians, diverse genres, stunning estate venue

picks.title: WorldPride Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major LGBTQI+ celebration with events, parades, and festivities across Amsterdam starting late July.
picks.why: City-wide Pride events, inclusive, free celebrations

picks.title: ARTIS Summer Nights
picks.blurb: Live emerging musicians perform in the bandstand at dusk among gibbons, flamingos & greenery. Every Saturday through August.

picks.title: ARTIS Summer Evenings
picks.blurb: Emerging musicians perform in the park's bandstand amid golden-hour light and wildlife.
picks.why: free admission with park entry, unique summer program

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: 20,000 visitors across 6+ stages celebrating urban culture: dancehall, hip-hop, house, disco. Live acts, DJs, food market.
picks.why: Major urban festival, 20K crowd, multi-stage lineup

picks.title: Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.blurb: Free open-air theatre with music, comedy, dance, youth shows all weekend long in central park.

picks.title: Vondelpark Openluchttheater
picks.blurb: Free outdoor performances spanning music, theatre, dance & comedy. Part of extensive summer program with 100+ shows.
picks.why: Free, diverse program, intimate park setting

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Prestigious photography exhibition showcasing award-winning images from photojournalists worldwide.

picks.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.
picks.why: Free · easy any time with kids

picks.title: 99dB
picks.blurb: Club night at OT301.

picks.title: AIFSS
picks.blurb: STIPP, Elias Mazian, MALLAURY, Varuna Agosti at Skatecafe.

picks.title: worldover by Jo FLM
picks.blurb: Underground techno & house with live instrumentation; part of Garage Noord's solid weekend programming.

picks.title: ARTIS-Aquarium
picks.blurb: Newly reopened aquarium with interactive exhibits teaching kids how water sustains life through play and discovery.
picks.why: Just reopened after major restoration; summer highlight

picks.title: Throwback | Back to 80s | 90s | 00s
picks.blurb: Retro-themed dance party returning for second edition; 80s, 90s and 00s throwbacks with doors at 5pm.
picks.why: Returning event, packed dancefloor first time

picks.title: Peter de Wolf (4+)
picks.blurb: Thrilling fairy tale performed at Vondelpark's outdoor theatre with beautiful piano accompaniment for young audiences.
picks.why: Family-friendly theatre in scenic summer setting

picks.title: SLAPPED w/ Mitch Oliver, Mustafa Ismaeel, Bákayan
picks.blurb: Deep house & progressive sounds; late-night session at historic 100-year-old event venue in city centre.
picks.why: Quality deep house lineup, extended hours

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists respond to Eye's colonial-era Indonesian and Surinamese films with new commissioned works.
picks.why: Fresh artistic responses to archival material · timely recko

picks.title: Mokumboot: Sloepje huren
picks.blurb: Rent a small boat to explore Amsterdam's waterways and canals at your own pace—perfect summer family adventure.
picks.why: Classic Amsterdam water experience; summer-perfect outing

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Keti Koti: Naar Ndakaaru zwemmen
picks.blurb: Celebrate and commemorate Keti Koti with stories, conversations, food, and music at this family-friendly cultural event.
picks.why: Meaningful cultural celebration with activities for all ages

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Micropia
picks.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Pure Markt
picks.blurb: An artisan food-and-craft market in a green park — small makers, slow food and a relaxed Sunday-stroll crowd in Frankendael.

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: World Pride celebration: queer filmmakers from around the globe in Eye's sweeping retrospective of freedom and diversity.
picks.why: World Pride Amsterdam · diverse international perspectives

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Ten Katemarkt
picks.blurb: A proper everyday neighbourhood market off the tourist track — heaped produce, street food and Oud-West locals doing the shop.

picks.title: Huis Marseille
picks.blurb: The city’s first photography museum, across two canal houses with a hidden garden — beautifully curated, never crowded.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.

picks.title: Kinderdijk Windmills
picks.blurb: Nineteen 18th-century windmills lined along the polders — the postcard Dutch landscape, a UNESCO site you can walk or cycle.
